mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate triggers a decrease in consumer spending, leading to lower demand for goods and services, which in turn produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in post-recovery periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the labor force participation rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to higher demand for goods and services, which in turn produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, leading to lower demand for goods and services, which in turn produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to higher inflation expectations, which in turn produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing-boom periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to lower discount rates applied to equity cash flows, which in turn produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the labor force participation rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to higher demand for goods and services, which in turn produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the consumer price index signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to higher inflation expectations, which in turn produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in inflationary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, leading to lower demand for goods and services, which in turn produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
